step_by_step_reasoning: The whiteboard shows a automated migration architecture. 1. A user node 'Migration Team' interacts with DynamoDB and Slack. 2. Slack triggers an EC2 instance representing the Migration Factory. 3. On-premises server replicates data to an EC2 Hot Replica and registers details with Application Discovery Service. 4. The EC2 Migration Factory coordinates App Discovery, CloudEndure, and bakes an AMI. 5. The baked AMI is shared across the account boundary (dashed line) to create a Shared AMI, which is then launched as a Target Account EC2 instance.

graph.name: Versent Automated Migration Factory
graph.notes: Reduces migration time from 4 hours to 10 minutes per server by automating agent data capture, block replication, and target-account launching.

nodes.name: Slack
nodes.notes: ChatOps interface used to initiate migration commands.

nodes.name: DynamoDB
nodes.notes: DATA_PEEK: Stores target account metadata, IPs, subnets, and tagging requirements.

nodes.name: Application Discovery Service
nodes.notes: WORKLOAD_PEEK: Captures TCP connections and performance utilization data.

nodes.name: Migration Factory EC2
nodes.notes: WORKLOAD_PEEK: Orchestration engine coordinating the end-to-end migration process.

nodes.name: EC2 Hot Replica
nodes.notes: Destination replica target for continuous data replication.

nodes.name: Bake AMI
nodes.notes: AMI created from the finalized replica.

nodes.name: Shared AMI
nodes.notes: AMI shared securely with the target AWS account.

nodes.name: Target Account EC2
nodes.notes: WORKLOAD_PEEK: Final migrated instance running in target VPC.

edges.notes: Gathers network and resource utilization metadata.
Instructs replication system to stop continuous copying.
Triggers AMI creation from hot replica.
